# UC-17.1.47 · Cisco ISE Backup Job Success and Operational-Data Backup Validation

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We make sure ISE's safety copy (the backup) actually completes every day. If the backup fails or skips a day, we ring the bell — without a recent backup, we couldn't restore ISE if something went wrong.*

---

## Description

Detects ISE backup-job failures, missed scheduled backups (>48h without a successful backup), and degraded backups (partial / repository validation failures). Covers config backup AND operational-data backup as separate streams.

## Value

ISE recovery requires a recent backup. Without a recent valid backup, restoration after PAN failure rebuilds the deployment from the last available, possibly weeks-old, backup — losing all policy work since. Auditing backup health is the primary control for ISE disaster-recovery readiness. Operational-data backup is required for forensic continuity of MnT events; missing this backup means audit data is gone if the MnT node fails.

## Implementation

Confirm *Administrative and Operational Audit* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller every 1h. Schedule the search every 4h, search window `-12h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `backup_type` for 12h.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Misconfigure backup repository credentials. Run a manual backup. ISE emits 51032 (failed). UC-17.1.47 fires showing the (backup_type, repository) with failures > 0.

### Negative scenario

Fix credentials. Successful backup emits 51031. Alert clears on next 4h schedule.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Backup repository configured (Administration > Maintenance > Repository).
- Scheduled backup configured (Administration > Maintenance > Backup & Restore > Schedule Backup).
- `backup_repos.csv` lookup with `repository`, `criticality`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Administrative and Operational Audit* category at INFO.

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 4h.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above. Confirm fire.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Backup Health":
- Days-since-success per backup_type tile.
- 90-day calendar view.
- Repository top-N failures.

Monthly attestation: ISE platform team signs off on backup success report.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
- **Repository auth failure** — Re-store credential.
- **Backup partial — operational data missing** — MnT disk full, cross-ref UC-17.1.29.
- **Schedule never fires** — Schedule disabled in UI.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (51030, 51031, 51032, 51033, 51034, 51035))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:backup_status")
| eval status = case(MessageCode=="51031" OR MessageCode=="51035", "success", MessageCode IN ("51032","51033","51034"), "failure", MessageCode=="51030", "started", true(), "snapshot")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_run, count(eval(status=="success")) as successes count(eval(status=="failure")) as failures latest(backup_size_mb) as size_mb by backup_type, backup_repository
| eval days_since_success = round((now() - last_run) / 86400, 1)
| where failures > 0 OR (status=="snapshot" AND days_since_success > 2) OR successes==0
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value: days since last successful backup per type. (2) Calendar view: 90-day backup success/failure grid. (3) Top-N: most-failed repositories.

## Known False Positives

- **First-backup-after-deployment** — Initial backup may take significantly longer than steady-state; suppress for 24h after first scheduled run.
- **Repository network outage** — A NAS/SFTP outage causes 51034 (repository validation failed); cross-reference network maintenance.
- **Partial backup during low-disk** — When MnT disk fills, operational-data backup falls back to partial. Cross-ref UC-17.1.29.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — Backup and Restore](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_maintain.html)
- [Cisco ISE — Operational Data Purge and Backup](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_monitoring_and_troubleshooting.html)
